title: Lesson Quiz: Creating and Managing a Budget
description: Assessment of the concept that Budget structure, tracking income and expenses.

choices.text: A plan for how money will be earned, spent, and saved
choices.feedback: Correct. A budget organizes money decisions by showing income, spending, and savings.

choices.text: A loan that must be repaid later
choices.feedback: A budget is a planning tool, not a type of loan.
A budget includes more than wants. It includes income, expenses, and priorities.

choices.text: Only a record of past purchases
choices.feedback: A budget can use past information, but it is mainly a plan for decisions.

questions.prompt: A balanced budget means income can cover planned expenses.

choices.feedback: A balanced budget does mean the planned income is enough to cover expenses.
Correct. A budget works best when income can support the planned categories.

questions.prompt: A student has $110 in income and $72 in planned expenses. How much money remains?

choices.feedback: Subtract the expenses from the income carefully.
This amount is too low. Subtract the planned expenses from the income carefully.
This total is too high after subtracting the planned expenses.

questions.prompt: Why is it important to total all expenses instead of checking only one category at a time?

choices.text: Because one category alone shows the full budget result
choices.feedback: One category does not show the total effect on the full budget.

choices.text: Because writing each category neatly matters more than checking the total
choices.feedback: Organization can help, but neat formatting alone does not explain why totaling all expenses matters.

choices.text: Because budget decisions should ignore savings
choices.feedback: Savings should be included when evaluating a budget.

choices.text: Because the full total shows whether the budget plan actually works
choices.feedback: Correct. Budget decisions should be based on the total financial picture.

questions.prompt: Budgeting can help a person justify spending decisions with evidence.

choices.feedback: Correct. A budget provides numerical evidence for whether a spending choice fits the plan.
Budgets do help support spending decisions with evidence.
